proposed_entries.title: ## Do-not-repeat: every UI plan must pin the exact mount-target file path
proposed_entries.body: ## Do-not-repeat: every UI plan must pin the exact mount-target file path

proposed_entries.title: Today's Ops dashboard MVP shipped to `index
proposed_entries.body: Today's Ops dashboard MVP shipped to `index.html` because the plan specification said "mounts into the existing Workbench shell" without naming the file. The subagent guessed `index.html`. The canonical Foundation lives at `site-studio/public/workbench-foundation.html`. Do not repeat: every UI plan must reference an exact absolute path for its mount target, never a noun phrase.

proposed_entries.title: ## Anti-pattern: declaring a shell frozen canonical without a cutover plan creates drift
proposed_entries.body: ## Anti-pattern: declaring a shell frozen canonical without a cutover plan creates drift

proposed_entries.title: The Workbench Foundation was declared "frozen canonical" in `docs/STUDIO-UI-FOUNDATION
proposed_entries.body: The Workbench Foundation was declared "frozen canonical" in `docs/STUDIO-UI-FOUNDATION.md` on 2026-05-04. No migration plan was attached. The next day, a new feature shipped into the legacy shell because that's where the working APIs lived. Anti-pattern: freeze plus working-old plus no-cutover equals duplicate-implementation drift the next day. A freeze without a cutover plan is decorative.

proposed_entries.title: The canonical Foundation renders 7 domain icons but only Sites and Plans are wired to live data
proposed_entries.body: The canonical Foundation renders 7 domain icons but only Sites and Plans are wired to live data. Brain, Components, Media, Intel, Admin all render UI shells but `buildXBench()` returns empty content. Logs and Approvals bottom panels show hardcoded fake entries. Right-rail Tools render pod containers but never populate. This is the operational reality of the canonical Foundation as of 2026-05-05.

proposed_entries.title: ## Learning: working architecture can diverge from locked architecture
proposed_entries.body: ## Learning: working architecture can diverge from locked architecture

proposed_entries.title: When the locked design doesn't match what works, the next agent will follow whichever surface produc
proposed_entries.body: When the locked design doesn't match what works, the next agent will follow whichever surface produces value. Today the canonical Foundation was locked, but `index.html` had every working API behind it — so today's Ops dashboard correctly went to where the wires were. This is not a violation; it's gravity. The fix is not to discipline the agent; it's to either wire the canonical or update the lock.
proposed_entries.auto_promote_reason: confidence >= 0.85 AND type 'bug-pattern' is in allowlist

proposed_entries.title: ## Rule: the Workbench Foundation R3 says Shay is never a left-nav domain
proposed_entries.body: ## Rule: the Workbench Foundation R3 says Shay is never a left-nav domain

proposed_entries.title: Per `docs/STUDIO-UI-FOUNDATION
proposed_entries.body: Per `docs/STUDIO-UI-FOUNDATION.md` Section 1 R3: Shay is ambient, not a nav item. Domains are things; Shay is the air. The current `index.html` left rail violates R3 with a Shay tab. Any future shell work must not add Shay as a top-level domain entry. Workshop UI is fine; making it a tab is not.

proposed_entries.title: ## Gap: Studio serves index
proposed_entries.body: ## Gap: Studio serves index.html as / by default; workbench-foundation.html is reachable but not the default

proposed_entries.title: ## Decision: research-only assessment shipped, three reconciliation paths documented
proposed_entries.body: ## Decision: research-only assessment shipped, three reconciliation paths documented

proposed_entries.title: Decided to ship a research-only assessment doc + a side-by-side viewer instead of a code merge
proposed_entries.body: Decided to ship a research-only assessment doc + a side-by-side viewer instead of a code merge. Three paths described and not chosen: Foundation wins (port everything in, deprecate index.html), index.html wins (rewrite the locked rules to match reality), Hybrid (Foundation as roadmap, index.html as live, gradual migration). Decision deferred to Fritz after observing both shells through the side-by-side viewer at `http://localhost:3334/shell-compare.html`.
